agent.name: synthesizer
agent.role: Merge, deduplicate, rank, and plan across all agent outputs.

findings.title: NestJS API build fails with TypeScript compilation errors in messaging service
findings.description: The build command `npm run build:api` (which invokes `nest build`) fails with 2 TypeScript errors in src/verticals/messaging/messaging/services/messaging.service.ts. The method `getUnreadCountForConversation` is called on lines 508 and 721 but is not defined in the MessagingService class. This blocks all deployments and prevents local development builds from completing.

proof_hooks.summary: TS2339 error on lines 508 and 721: Property 'getUnreadCountForConversation' does not exist on type 'MessagingService'.
proof_hooks.error_text:
src/verticals/messaging/messaging/services/messaging.service.ts:508:36 - error TS2339: Property 'getUnreadCountForConversation' does not exist on type 'MessagingService'.

src/verticals/messaging/messaging/services/messaging.service.ts:721:36 - error TS2339: Property 'getUnreadCountForConversation' does not exist on type 'MessagingService'.

Found 2 error(s).

proof_hooks.summary: Build script invokes nest build without --transpile-only flag, so strict TypeScript checking halts on errors.

suggested_fix.approach: Implement missing getUnreadCountForConversation method or refactor calls

findings.title: CI/CD pipeline does not build or test the API; only web typecheck and build are validated
findings.description: The CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml) runs database migrations, validates the Prisma schema, and builds the web app, but does NOT attempt to build the API. This means TypeScript errors and build failures in apps/api/ are not caught until deployment, and deployments fail without warning in CI logs. The build failure in MessagingService would not be detected without manual testing.

proof_hooks.summary: Steps: checkout, setup node, install deps, generate prisma, run migrations, validate API prisma, typecheck web, build web. No 'Build API' step.

suggested_fix.approach: Add Build API and optional Test API steps to ci.yml after Prisma validation

findings.title: API TypeScript strict mode enabled but hundreds of pre-existing type errors prevent clean builds
findings.description: The API has 'strict': true in tsconfig, but ts_errors.log, ts_errors_v2.log through ts_errors_v7.log exist in apps/api/, indicating known accumulation of type errors (666+ errors mentioned in AGENTS.md). Currently, builds invoke `nest build` which fails on first error. Developers are forced to either fix all type errors or use a workaround.

proof_hooks.summary: TypeScript strict mode is enabled, but hundreds of violations exist.
Pre-existing type error log from past build attempts.

suggested_fix.approach: Phase 1: Run typecheck without --transpile-only and bucket errors. Phase 2: Fix P0/P1 type errors. Phase 3: Enforce in CI. Phase 4: Gradual cleanup.

findings.title: Web frontend has global _error page but no React Error Boundary component for client-side crashes
findings.description: The Next.js _error.tsx page handles server-side errors and 404s, but there is no React Error Boundary component wrapping the application tree. If a React component throws an error during render, the error boundary would catch it; without it, the browser console shows an error and the component is unmounted without user feedback. This leaves users looking at a blank screen or a confusing state.

proof_hooks.summary: Global error page exists for SSR and routing errors. But no client-side error boundary wraps components.

steps: Trigger a React error: e.g., throw new Error('test') in a component render
Observe: browser console shows error, component tree unmounts, no fallback UI shown
proof_hooks.summary: No error boundary catches component render errors.

suggested_fix.approach: Create ErrorBoundary component in apps/web/src/components/ and wrap <Component> in _app.tsx

findings.title: API health check endpoint referenced in deploy.yml but may not be implemented in NestJS app
findings.description: The deploy.yml workflow checks API health by querying /api/health (line 88), but the NestJS app code does not show a health check endpoint. The health check is critical for the deployment pipeline to confirm the API is ready after deployment. If the endpoint is missing or returns wrong status codes, the deployment verification will timeout and fail.

proof_hooks.summary: Deployment workflow queries /api/health. No implementation of this endpoint found in NestJS app.

suggested_fix.approach: Create a HealthController with GET /health endpoint. Inject services to check DB and Redis connectivity. Return 200 if all OK, 503 otherwise.

findings.title: Environment variables are not validated at startup; missing or invalid vars could cause runtime crashes hours after boot
findings.description: The API main.ts logs a warning if SENTRY_DSN is missing, but does not validate required variables like JWT_SECRET, DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, or AWS credentials at startup. This means if a deploy is misconfigured, the app boots successfully but crashes later when it first tries to use a missing credential. Critical variables should be validated synchronously at bootstrap and fail fast.

proof_hooks.summary: Only SENTRY_DSN and ALLOWED_ORIGINS checked. No validation of JWT_SECRET, DATABASE_URL, credentials, etc.
Many required variables documented but no startup validation ensures they are present.

suggested_fix.approach: Use ConfigModule with validation. Define required vars in a schema. Call app.get(ConfigService).validate() at startup.

findings.title: Next.js web app does not validate required NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL at build time
findings.description: The web app references NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL in several places (e.g., apps/web/src/lib/api/error.ts), but there is no build-time validation to ensure it is set. If the var is missing at build or runtime, the app will use the fallback 'http://localhost:3003/api', which may be incorrect in staging/production and cause silent API failures.

proof_hooks.summary: Falls back to localhost:3003 if NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL not defined.

suggested_fix.approach: Add validation in next.config.js before module.exports: check env var presence in production.

findings.title: API logging uses NestJS built-in Logger (console-based) instead of structured JSON logging
findings.description: The API logs errors and status messages using @nestjs/common Logger (lines in redis.ts, redis-io.adapter.ts, main.ts), which outputs plain text to stdout. In production, structured JSON logging (e.g., via Winston, Pino) makes it easier to parse, filter, and aggregate logs in centralized logging systems (CloudWatch, DataDog, Splunk, etc.). Plain text logs are harder to parse programmatically.

proof_hooks.summary: Uses `private readonly logger = new Logger(RedisService.name)`; outputs plain text to console.

suggested_fix.approach: Add Winston/Pino, create logging module, gradually migrate services to use it.

findings.title: Sentry error reporting is not configured; production errors are not sent to external service
findings.description: The main.ts warns if SENTRY_DSN is not set, but there is no Sentry SDK integration in the API. This means unhandled exceptions, caught errors, and other observability signals are not sent to Sentry for alerting and debugging. In production, you are blind to post-deployment errors until a user reports them.

proof_hooks.summary: Logs warning if SENTRY_DSN not set, but no active integration.

suggested_fix.approach: Install Sentry, configure in main.ts, create exception filter, set DSN in production.

findings.title: Production Docker compose config (docker-compose.prod.yml) is referenced in deploy.yml but not found in repo
findings.description: The deploy.yml workflow references docker/docker-compose.prod.yml (line 79), but only docker-compose.yml exists in the repo. The prod config may be intentionally excluded from version control (e.g., it contains secrets or is managed separately), or it may be missing and deployments will fail. This ambiguity needs a human decision.

proof_hooks.summary: References docker/docker-compose.prod.yml which is not in the repo.

suggested_fix.approach: Human decision: verify docker-compose.prod.yml location and whether it should be version-controlled.

findings.title: Database migration rollback plan is not documented; failed deployments could leave database in inconsistent state
findings.description: The deploy workflow runs `npm run db:migrate:deploy` as part of the deployment. Prisma migrations are forward-only; there is no built-in rollback mechanism. If a migration fails mid-deployment, the database may be left in a partially-migrated state and the API may not start. A rollback and recovery plan should be documented.

suggested_fix.approach: Add migration rollback runbook to README. Consider automated backups before migrations.

findings.title: Content Security Policy in web app uses unsafe-inline for scripts and styles; overly permissive for production
findings.description: The next.config.js defines a Content-Security-Policy header with script-src 'unsafe-inline' and 'unsafe-eval' (lines 35), which allows any inline script and dynamically-generated code. This defeats CSP's purpose of preventing XSS attacks. The policy should be tightened to use nonces or strict script-src before launch.

proof_hooks.summary: CSP allows unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval scripts; should use nonces or remove for production.

suggested_fix.approach: Implement nonce-based CSP in next.config.js and update inline script handling.

findings.title: No automated rollback strategy if deployment health check fails
findings.description: The deploy workflow waits 120 seconds for API health check to pass. If it times out, the step fails and marks the deployment as failed, but the deployed container is left running. There is no automatic rollback to the previous version. If a deployment is broken, manual intervention is required.

proof_hooks.summary: If health check fails, job fails but no rollback is triggered.

suggested_fix.approach: Add rollback step to deploy job that reverses deployment on health check failure.

findings.title: Missing method: getUnreadCountForConversation in MessagingService
findings.description: MessagingService.sendMessage (line 508) and MessagingService.markAsRead (line 721) call this.getUnreadCountForConversation(), but this method is never defined in the service. The method exists as a public getter (line 885-913, getUnreadCount), but the called signature expects a conversationId and userId parameter pair. This is a compile-time blocker that prevents the entire API from building.

proof_hooks.error_text: Property 'getUnreadCountForConversation' does not exist on type 'MessagingService'

suggested_fix.approach: Define the missing method in MessagingService with correct signature accepting conversationId and userId
steps: Add method after getUnreadCount method (around line 914):
Run npm run build:api to verify the fix

history.notes: Discovered during runtime-bug-hunter audit via build output

findings.title: Potential null dereference in conversation deletion without soft-delete
findings.description: deleteConversation checks that the user is a participant but performs a hard delete without soft-delete support. If delete fails mid-cascade (e.g., foreign key constraints on messages), the conversation state becomes undefined but no error propagates to the caller. Additionally, the method returns success before confirming the actual database state.

suggested_fix.approach: Implement soft-delete pattern or wrap delete in transaction with error handling
steps: - Add deletedAt?: DateTime field to Conversation in prisma schema
- Update deleteConversation to set deletedAt: new Date()
- Update all conversation queries to filter: { deletedAt: null }
- Check return value before returning success

history.notes: Identified via code analysis of deleteConversation method

findings.title: Unsafe profile access in follows.service.ts without existence check
findings.description: followUser increments profile counters (line 78-86) but profile.update() may fail silently if the profile doesn't exist. Per the AGENTS.md guidance, some legacy/seeded users may not have profiles. The Promise.all on lines 77-86 will reject if either profile is missing, causing the entire follow to fail after already creating the follow record, resulting in inconsistent state.

suggested_fix.approach: Wrap profile updates in try-catch or use updateMany with upsert
steps: // Log and optionally undo follow creation
this.logger.warn('Failed to update profiles after follow', err);
Or: Use updateMany with condition: where: { userId, profile: { not: null } }

history.notes: Identified via code analysis of followUser method

findings.title: Race condition in idempotency check for checkout (orders.service.ts)
findings.description: checkout() checks for existing orders by idempotencyKey (lines 79-90), then proceeds to create new orders. Between the check and the create, another request with the same idempotencyKey could race in, violating idempotency. The current logic returns the old orders if they exist, but if a concurrent request passes the findMany, both will create orders, resulting in duplicate charges.

suggested_fix.approach: Move idempotency check inside database transaction with unique constraint
steps: Add unique constraint to Orders table (buyerId, idempotencyKey) in Prisma schema
Wrap entire checkout flow in this.prisma.$transaction
Use upsert pattern or catch unique constraint violation and return existing order

history.notes: Identified via code analysis of checkout method

findings.title: Silent notification failure in follows.service.ts
findings.description: Notification creation is swallowed with .catch(() => {}). If notifications are critical for UX (e.g., user never learns of new followers), this masks silent failures. No logging of the error, and no metrics to detect systemic issues. Users will not know they have new followers.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace silent catch with logging and metrics
steps: this.logger.warn(`Failed to create follow notification for user ${dto.followingId}`, err);
Consider whether notifications should fail the entire follow operation

history.notes: Identified via code analysis of error handling pattern

findings.title: Unsafe access to messages[0] in markAsRead without bounds check
findings.description: After marking messages as read, markAsRead retrieves the conversation and accesses conversation.messages[0] (line 734) without checking if the array exists or has elements. If the conversation has no messages (e.g., deleted just before this call), this will be null and cause type errors in the response.

suggested_fix.approach: Add null-coalescing before accessing array element

history.notes: Identified via code analysis of array access pattern

findings.title: Missing await on rate limiter check (messaging.service.ts:445)
findings.description: sendMessage calls this.rateLimiter.isAllowed (line 445) without await. If isAllowed is an async method, the Promise will resolve immediately (non-awaited), and rate limiting will be skipped entirely. Users can spam unlimited messages.

suggested_fix.approach: Add await to rate limiter check
steps: Verify message-rate-limiter.service.ts confirms isAllowed is async

history.notes: Identified via code analysis of async/await pattern

findings.title: Unsafe property access in message sender mapping (messaging.service.ts:500)
findings.description: sendMessage maps message.sender without null check (line 500-503). If sender is not included in the query result (unlikely but possible after refactoring), accessing message.sender.id will throw. Also uses unsafe any-casts which mask type errors.

suggested_fix.approach: Remove any-casts and add existence check
steps: Line 500: Add check: if (!message.sender) throw new Error('Message sender not populated in query');
Remove any-casts and use proper typing instead

history.notes: Identified via code analysis of type casting pattern

findings.title: Unhandled promise rejection in useMessaging hook error callback
findings.description: fetchConversations calls onError callback (line 308) after catching an error, then re-throws (line 309). If caller doesn't handle the thrown error or if onError is undefined, the error swallows silently in some component trees, making debugging difficult.

suggested_fix.approach: Clarify error handling flow in hook
steps: Either: (1) Remove the re-throw and let onError handle it
Or: (2) Log error with console.error before re-throw
Or: (3) Ensure all callers wrap in try-catch and handle appropriately

history.notes: Identified via code analysis of hook error handling

findings.title: Unsafe access to response.message in createConversation hook
findings.description: createConversation assumes response.message exists (line 333), but the API may return null if no initial message was sent. This will cause the UI to render undefined for lastMessage.

suggested_fix.approach: Add null coalescing for response.message
steps: Line 333: Replace response.message with response.message || null

history.notes: Identified via code analysis of API response handling

findings.title: Unreachable code in markAsRead hook (useMessaging.ts:427-447)
findings.description: markAsRead has a condition on line 427: if (!socket || !isConnected). The logic flow is unclear - if both are false, code falls through to line 449 (emitWithAck), but emitWithAck checks if socket is not connected and rejects. This creates potentially dead code in the HTTP fallback branch if socket is null but isConnected is true (stale state).

suggested_fix.approach: Clarify control flow with explicit if-else

history.notes: Identified via code analysis of conditional logic

findings.title: Unsafe access to data.conversationId in setMessages state update
findings.description: markAsRead updates messages with setMessages((prev) => { const conversationMessages = prev[data.conversationId] || [] }). If data.conversationId is undefined or doesn't exist, prev[undefined] will be undefined, and the || [] fallback is safe but indicates potential data inconsistency that should be validated.

suggested_fix.approach: Add validation for required data fields
steps: Add early validation: if (!data.conversationId) throw new Error('conversationId required')
Or log warning: if (!data.conversationId) logger.warn('Missing conversationId in markAsRead event')

history.notes: Identified via code analysis of state update pattern

findings.title: Potential N+1 query in messaging.service.getConversations with nested includes
findings.description: The `getConversations` method includes `participant1`, `participant2`, and their profiles in a findMany call with pagination. If conversations table is large, fetching all participants + profiles for 20 items may be inefficient without indexed lookups.

proof_hooks.summary: getConversations includes nested User and Profile data

findings.suggested_fix: Add database indexes on participant1Id and participant2Id in Conversation table. Consider pagination cursor with indexed ordering. Profile select should limit to minimal fields (id, username, displayName, avatarUrl).

findings.title: Missing indexes on frequently-used WHERE columns in messaging and follows tables
findings.description: Grep analysis shows heavy use of participant1Id, participant2Id, followerId, followingId in WHERE clauses. These columns must have indexes to prevent full table scans as data grows.

proof_hooks.summary: Conversation lookups by participant IDs without visible indexes
proof_hooks.query_text: SELECT * FROM conversations WHERE participant1Id = ? OR participant2Id = ?
SELECT * FROM follows WHERE followerId = ? AND followingId = ?

findings.suggested_fix: Add Prisma indexes: @db.Index([participant1Id]), @db.Index([participant2Id]) on Conversation; @db.Index([followerId]), @db.Index([followingId]) on Follow; @db.Index([conversationId]) on Message.

findings.title: Messaging service N+1: follows.service calls blockingService.isBlocked() and extra findUnique in loop
findings.description: In `followUser`, code checks target user existence (findUnique), then checks blocking (calls blockingService), then checks existing follow (findUnique). Each step triggers separate queries. For batch follow operations, this scales linearly.

proof_hooks.summary: Sequential checks in followUser causing multiple queries

findings.suggested_fix: Combine checks into single Prisma transaction or use raw SQL batch: SELECT user, follow, blocked in one query. Investigate blockingService.isBlocked() to ensure it also caches or batches.

findings.title: Frontend: Feed component may re-fetch on every dependency change without debounce
findings.description: useFeed hook loads feed on mount and when feedType/limit changes. No evidence of dependency stable references or memoization preventing unnecessary re-fetches.

proof_hooks.summary: useFeed loads on feedType/limit change without memoization guard

findings.suggested_fix: Wrap feedType/limit in useMemo in parent. Or use AbortController to cancel in-flight requests on dependency change. Verify FeedTabs component doesn't recreate feedType object on every render.

findings.title: Cache service falls back to in-memory with no size limit, potential memory leak
findings.description: InMemoryCache in cache.service.ts stores entries indefinitely until expiration. No cleanup() call scheduled, and no max-size limit. Long-running API could accumulate stale entries.
findings.suggested_fix: Schedule periodic cache cleanup every 5 min: `setInterval(() => this.inMemoryCache.cleanup(), 300000)`. Or implement LRU eviction with max size (e.g., 10,000 entries).

findings.title: Cache service does not handle Redis connection failures gracefully in get/set
findings.description: Cache.get/set catch Redis errors and fall back to in-memory, but no metric/log to track fallback frequency. If Redis is flaky, client could degrade to in-memory without visibility.

proof_hooks.summary: Redis fallback catches error but no visibility

findings.suggested_fix: Emit observability event on Redis fallback. Add Prometheus counter `cache_redis_fallback_total`. Set circuit breaker: after 5 consecutive Redis failures, skip Redis for 60 seconds.

findings.title: Wallet.service queries all transactions without pagination or filtering
findings.description: getWallet includes full user + profile select, but no evidence of transaction list limiting or cursor pagination in wallet endpoints. Could load 1000s of transactions on large wallets.

proof_hooks.summary: getWallet includes user but transaction pagination not visible

findings.suggested_fix: Split wallet fetch from transaction fetch. Return transaction summary (count, recent 10) in wallet response. Add separate paginated endpoint for transactions with cursor.

findings.title: API client always sends withCredentials=true, adds latency to preflight requests
findings.description: apiClient in apps/web/src/lib/api/client.ts sets withCredentials globally. This causes CORS preflight OPTIONS requests for every cross-origin POST/PUT, even if cookies aren't needed.
findings.suggested_fix: Only set withCredentials=true for auth endpoints. For public API calls, omit or set false. Use request interceptor to conditionally add withCredentials based on endpoint.

findings.title: Frontend: Marketplace page creates new hooks with unfetched dependencies on every render
findings.description: marketplaceIndex.tsx calls useMarketplace() on every render, and load() is called in useEffect with deps [debouncedQuery, typeFilter, category, minPrice, maxPrice, groupId]. If groupId is object or complex state, causes refetch.

proof_hooks.summary: useEffect dependencies include router.query.groupId which can change reference

findings.suggested_fix: Use useMemo to memoize groupId value. Or track previous groupId with useRef to prevent re-fetch on router ready.

findings.title: Frontend: Images in pages lack optimization, no Next.js Image component used consistently
findings.description: Marketplace, events, and groups pages use raw `` tags with hardcoded sizes. No lazy loading, no responsive images, no format optimization (webp, avif).

proof_hooks.summary: Raw img tags without Next.js Image optimization

findings.suggested_fix: Replace all `` with Next.js `` component. Add `priority` to above-fold images, enable lazy load for rest. Set `sizes` for responsive widths.

findings.title: Stripe SDK imported but stripe-connect.service appears disabled
findings.description: apps/api/src/core/monetization/services/gigs-payment.service.disabled.ts imports Stripe but is never used. stripe-connect.service likely handles payments instead. Unused dependency adds bundle size.
findings.suggested_fix: If gigs-payment.service is truly disabled, remove .disabled.ts file. Verify stripe-connect.service is production code. If not using Stripe, remove from package.json.

findings.title: Messaging hook creates Socket.io connections without connection pooling
findings.description: useMessaging hook in apps/web creates new Socket.io connection on every mount. Multiple components using this hook will create multiple connections to same server.

proof_hooks.summary: Socket.io client created in hook without pooling

findings.suggested_fix: Create SocketProvider context at app root, share single Socket.io instance. Use useMessaging as consumer hook. Reduces server connection count and memory per client.

findings.title: follows.service denormalizes follower/following counts but no transaction atomicity on update
findings.description: followUser updates participant profile counts with separate Promise.all queries. If one fails, counts become out of sync. No database-level triggers to maintain consistency.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap in Prisma transaction: `prisma.$transaction([...])`. Or create database trigger on Follow insert/delete to auto-increment/decrement profile counts.

findings.title: Rating limiter service may not scale to distributed deployments
findings.description: RateLimitService uses in-memory state to track user actions. In multi-instance setup, each instance has separate rate limit, user could bypass by hitting different servers.
findings.suggested_fix: Migrate RateLimitService to use Redis with keys like `rate:post:create:{userId}`. Use INCR with EXPIRE. Implement sliding window or token bucket in Redis.

findings.title: Missing database indexes on post.authorId, message.senderId, comment.postId (common JOINs)
findings.description: Pattern analysis shows posts are queried by authorId, messages by senderId/conversationId, comments by postId. No visible @db.Index decorators for these foreign keys.

proof_hooks.summary: Posts by author without visible index
proof_hooks.query_text: SELECT * FROM posts WHERE authorId = ? ORDER BY createdAt DESC LIMIT 20

proof_hooks.summary: Messages by sender without visible index
proof_hooks.query_text: SELECT * FROM messages WHERE senderId = ? AND conversationId = ?

findings.suggested_fix: Add @db.Index([authorId]) to Post, @db.Index([senderId]) to Message, @db.Index([postId]) to Comment. Composite indexes for common filters: @db.Index([senderId, createdAt]).

findings.title: Prisma client instantiated without connection pooling configuration
findings.description: apps/api/src/core/database/prisma.service.ts likely instantiates PrismaClient without explicit `pool_connections` or `connection_timeout` settings. Defaults may be insufficient for high load.
findings.suggested_fix: Set DATABASE_URL with query params: `?schema=public&connection_limit=20&pool_timeout=10`. Or use `prisma.schema.prisma` datasource to configure pool explicitly in code.

findings.title: AWS SES email service may incur high costs if sending to unvalidated addresses
findings.description: Email service uses @aws-sdk/client-ses without apparent rate limiting or validation. If app sends to typos/invalid emails, SES charges per email sent.

proof_hooks.summary: Email service sends without visible validation

findings.suggested_fix: Validate email addresses before sending (RFC 5322 regex or library like email-validator). Deduplicate batch sends by recipient. Implement SES reputation monitoring.

findings.title: Next.js image remotePatterns allows wildcard subdomains, may bypass CDN optimization
findings.description: next.config.js permits `*.s3.amazonaws.com` and `*.cloudfront.net` with wildcard. Next.js Image optimization requires known domain patterns for effective caching.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace wildcard with specific bucket: `{ protocol: 'https', hostname: 'my-bucket.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' }`. Use CloudFront distribution as canonical CDN. Enable Image Cache-Control headers.

findings.title: Query: scheduling unknown frequency of scheduled posts processor
findings.description: scheduled-posts.service.ts exists with queries on ScheduledPost table, but frequency of background job processor is unknown. Could cause missed scheduled posts or wasteful polling.

proof_hooks.summary: What is the scheduled post processor frequency?

findings.suggested_fix: Verify @Cron decorator or job queue (Bull, RabbitMQ). Ensure frequency is at most once per minute and uses indexes for `dueAt <= NOW()` query.

findings.title: Query: Mux video API usage metering and cost tracking not visible
findings.description: Mux SDK is integrated (@mux/mux-node), but no evidence of request logging or cost tracking. Mux charges by minutes viewed; unbounded usage could cause bill shock.

proof_hooks.summary: Is Mux usage metered and tracked?

findings.suggested_fix: Log every Mux API call with request size. Set up Mux cost alerts in dashboard. Implement cache for thumbnails/previews to avoid redundant encoding.

findings.title: Production secrets committed to git in .env file
findings.description: The .env file at repository root contains real production credentials including database passwords, JWT secrets, Redis passwords, AWS keys, Stripe live keys, Mux tokens, and email credentials. This file is tracked in git history and accessible to anyone with repo access.

findings.title: SQL Injection in $queryRaw without parameterization
findings.description: Two database query endpoints use Prisma's $queryRaw with string interpolation for user-controlled parameters (userId, limit). While Prisma's template tag syntax usually provides protection, the code uses direct ${} template literal interpolation which bypasses Prisma's parameterization.

findings.title: Missing COOKIE_SECURE environment variable allows cookie transmission over HTTP
findings.description: The auth.controller.ts sets secure cookie flag based on `process.env.COOKIE_SECURE === 'true'`, but the .env file does not define this variable. When undefined, cookies are set with secure=false, allowing them to be transmitted over unencrypted HTTP connections.

findings.title: Over-exposed user data in API responses leaks private fields
findings.description: The sanitizeUser function in auth.service.ts only removes passwordHash and googleId, but returns user objects with unreviewed fields. User responses include sensitive data like wallet balances, full names, email addresses, and verification status that may not be appropriate for all contexts.

findings.title: IDOR in wallet endpoints—no ownership verification
findings.description: The WalletController.getWallet() method uses @GetUser('id') to get the current user but does not verify the user actually owns the wallet being accessed. An attacker could potentially craft requests to access other users' wallets if route parameters are not properly scoped.

findings.title: CORS credentials exposed with loose origin validation
findings.description: The main.ts enables CORS with `credentials: true` and parses ALLOWED_ORIGINS from env. However, the allowed origins list includes localhost URLs (3000, 3001, 3004) for development. If ALLOWED_ORIGINS is misconfigured or defaults are used in production, it opens the API to cross-origin requests with cookies.

findings.title: Account enumeration vulnerability via forgot-password and email verification endpoints
findings.description: The forgot-password and resend-verification endpoints return the same message ('If that email is in our system...') whether the email exists or not. However, timing attacks could still enumerate accounts if email lookup is faster than email send. Additionally, no account enumeration protection like exponential backoff or account lockout is present.

findings.title: Google OAuth redirect validation incomplete—OPEN_REDIRECT risk if FRONTEND_URL env not set
findings.description: In auth.controller.ts line 101-107, the googleAuthCallback validates that FRONTEND_URL exists but only returns 503 error if missing. It doesn't validate that FRONTEND_URL is a valid, trusted URL. An attacker who compromises the deployment environment could set FRONTEND_URL to a malicious site to redirect users after OAuth login.

findings.title: No rate limiting on login endpoint—brute force vulnerability
findings.description: The login endpoint (POST /api/auth/login) has no @Throttle decorator. An attacker can make unlimited login attempts to brute force user passwords using the seed accounts (admin@embr.app:test1234, creator@embr.app:test1234, user@embr.app:test1234) or any known email.

findings.title: Password reset token leak in email—user ID embedded in plaintext token
findings.description: In auth.service.ts line 455-456, the password reset token is constructed as `${user.id}.${randomBytes(32)}` and sent in plaintext in the email. The user ID portion is not secret and can be extracted by anyone who intercepts or views the email.

findings.title: Seed account credentials documented in AGENTS.md allow unauthorized access
findings.description: The AGENTS.md file at the repo root documents seed account credentials: admin@embr.app / test1234, creator@embr.app / test1234, user@embr.app / test1234. These are used for development/testing but are hardcoded and persistent. If seed data is not reset in production, these accounts can be used to gain privileged access.

findings.title: Inconsistent authentication terminology
findings.description: Sign-in flow uses 'Sign in' and 'Signing in...' but home page uses 'Sign in to continue'. Signup page consistently uses 'Create account' but password reset may use different terminology. No standardized copy set for auth actions across app.

proof_hooks.summary: Home page uses 'Sign in to continue'

suggested_fix.approach: Create copy constants in a shared location (e.g., libs/copy or theme/strings). Define single source of truth for: 'Sign in', 'Sign out', 'Create account', 'Loading states' across all flows.

findings.title: Broken navigation flow: No back button or escape route on auth pages
findings.description: Auth pages (login, signup, forgot-password, reset-password) have no consistent way to go back. Users can navigate forward but can't easily return to home or discover without using browser back button. Violates common UX pattern of providing escape routes.

proof_hooks.summary: Login page has no back navigation
Signup page has no back navigation

suggested_fix.approach: Add back button or home link at top of auth pages. Pattern: 'Back to Home' link or breadcrumb showing path. Consistent with ProtectedPageShell breadcrumb pattern used elsewhere.

findings.title: Missing loading state on profile page
findings.description: Profile page shows 'Loading profile' while auth loading, but does not show loading state while posts are fetching. If posts take time to load, user sees blank 'Recent Posts' section with only text 'Loading posts...' instead of skeleton or animated placeholder.

proof_hooks.summary: Profile shows text-only loading for posts

suggested_fix.approach: Use PageState component or skeleton loader when isLoadingPosts=true. Consistent with Feed component's loading pattern (shows spinner + text).

findings.title: Inconsistent empty state messaging across list views
findings.description: Different list pages use different empty state copy: Marketplace: 'Nothing to see here yet', Events: 'No upcoming events yet', Feed: 'No posts yet'. No consistent pattern or tone. Should use unified empty state component.

suggested_fix.approach: Create a reusable EmptyState component that accepts context. Standardize copy: '[Noun] is empty', 'Be the first to [action]', CTA button. Use across all list pages.

findings.title: Missing alt text on decorative images and avatars
findings.description: Several images missing descriptive alt text: marketplace.tsx has alt='' on listing images, events page has alt='' on host avatars. OrderCard.tsx explicitly uses alt='' which signals to screen readers image is decorative, but these images are content.

proof_hooks.summary: Marketplace order image with empty alt
Event host avatar with empty alt

suggested_fix.approach: Add descriptive alt text to all content images. Pattern: 'Product name' for listings, 'Username's avatar' for user images. Keep decorative SVG icons as alt='' but verify with screen reader.

findings.title: Dead-end page: Marketplace checkout flow incomplete
findings.description: Marketplace has /marketplace/checkout/index.tsx route but no clear entry point visible. Create listing page doesn't wire to checkout. No 'Buy Now' flow documented. Page exists but is unreachable from purchase flow—creates confusion.

suggested_fix.approach: Wire ListingCard onClick to navigate to listing detail page. Add 'Buy Now' button on detail that routes to checkout with ?listingId=X. Ensure checkout page has clear flow: review > payment > confirmation.

findings.title: Messaging compose modal lacks clear confirmation feedback
findings.description: DMInbox ComposeModal closes after selecting a user but doesn't show confirmation that conversation was created or loaded. User sees spinner but no success message. If network is slow, user may not know if action succeeded.

suggested_fix.approach: Show toast notification on successful conversation creation: 'Conversation started with @username'. Keep loading state visible until conversation appears in list.

findings.title: Generic error messages don't guide user action
findings.description: Feed error: 'Unable to load feed' + button 'Try Again'. DMInbox error handling exists but no user-facing error messages shown for common failures (network, permission, etc). GigDiscovery shows error text but no retry mechanism.

suggested_fix.approach: Standardize error messages: 'Network error—please check your connection', 'Permission denied—sign in again', 'Server error—try again in a moment'. Add retry button to all error states. Use ErrorBoundary to catch and display.

findings.title: Custom button implementations lack proper ARIA labels and keyboard support
findings.description: Settings page has custom Toggle component with proper ARIA. But many pages use inline button elements with only title attribute (e.g., 'New Message' button in DMInbox). No aria-label, no keyboard nav guidance. Role attribute missing from clickable divs.

suggested_fix.approach: Add aria-label to all button-like elements. Use <button> tag instead of <div role='button'>. Test keyboard nav: Tab, Space/Enter to activate. Use existing Button component from @embr/ui where possible.

findings.title: Missing route: Gigs booking page doesn't display gig detail
findings.description: Route exists: /gigs/[id].tsx and /gigs/booking/[gigId].tsx. But no route visible to display individual gig detail before booking. GigDiscovery clicks navigate to router.push(/gigs/{id}) but gigs/[id].tsx page may not exist or is unimplemented.

suggested_fix.approach: Implement /gigs/[id].tsx page showing gig detail, description, budget, requirements, similar gigs. Add 'Apply' CTA that navigates to booking form. Wire booking form to submission endpoint.

findings.title: Mixed tone in CTAs: 'Join the Feed' vs 'Go to feed' vs 'Continue to feed'
findings.description: Home page: 'Join the Feed', 'Create'. About page: 'Create'. Create page itself: none. Inconsistent CTA tone—some are inviting ('Join'), some are functional ('Go'). Should standardize verb + target across entire app.

suggested_fix.approach: Define CTA library: Primary = action verb + target (e.g., 'Post Now', 'Start Messaging', 'List Item'). Secondary = neutral (e.g., 'Cancel', 'Skip'). Tertiary = info (e.g., 'Learn more'). Update all pages to use consistent pattern.

findings.title: No visible onboarding or feature discovery for new users
findings.description: Home page redirects authenticated users directly to /feed. No onboarding flow. New users see feed but don't know how to create profile, add avatar, enable creator mode, etc. Gigs, marketplace, music features are hidden in nav but user doesn't know they exist or how to enable them.

suggested_fix.approach: Create onboarding component shown on first login: 'Complete your profile' → 'Enable creator mode' → 'Explore features'. Use localStorage to track if user has completed onboarding. Show optional tooltips on nav items (Gigs, Marketplace, Music) on first visit.

findings.title: Post creation doesn't clear state or show success confirmation
findings.description: PostCreator successfully creates post and redirects to /post/[id]. But no toast or success message shown. User might not realize their action succeeded if redirect is slow. No breadcrumb trail back to feed from success state.

proof_hooks.summary: Post creation redirects but no feedback

suggested_fix.approach: Show toast: 'Post published!' before redirect. Or show success page with 'View post' and 'Back to feed' buttons. Ensure redirect happens after user sees confirmation.

findings.title: Form inputs lack associated labels in some components
findings.description: EventForm and marketplace sell form have input fields but many are missing visible labels. Placeholder text alone is insufficient for accessibility. Screen reader users won't know what each field represents.

suggested_fix.approach: Add <label> elements to all input fields. Ensure label is associated via htmlFor attribute. Keep placeholder as helper text, not replacement for label. Use existing Input component which likely has label support.

findings.title: Error messages don't provide recovery instructions
findings.description: Settings page: 'Could not save' with description 'Please try again.' doesn't explain why (network? server error? validation?). PostCreator shows error text but no guidance on how to fix.

suggested_fix.approach: Provide context-specific error recovery: 'Connection lost—check your internet', 'File too large—try a smaller image', 'Field required—fill in all fields'. Show actionable next step.

top_5_commits.title: fix: Missing method: getUnreadCountForConversation in M
fix: NestJS API build fails with TypeScript compilation
fix: Production secrets committed to git in .env file
fix: SQL Injection in $queryRaw without parameterizatio
fix: Missing COOKIE_SECURE environment variable allows
